Assessment Item: Secure coding standards are formally defined
Comments: Draft standard circulated; ratification and enforcement are still in progress.
Description (What this question Actually: Whether the team has a documented, agreed secure-coding standard that engineers must follow.
Examples / Evidence for teams to referen: Ratified secure-coding standard in the team handbook or wiki.

Assessment Item: Developers are trained on the OWASP Top 10
Comments: All engineers complete annual secure-coding training, tracked in the LMS.
Description (What this question Actually: Whether engineers receive regular training on the most common web vulnerabilities.
Examples / Evidence for teams to referen: LMS completion report; training attendance records.

Assessment Item: Semgrep SAST scans run in the CI/CD pipeline
Comments: We are still evaluating the tool right now and a full pipeline rollout is planned for next quarter.
Description (What this question Actually: Whether automated static analysis runs on every pull request and blocks merges on high-severity findings.
Examples / Evidence for teams to referen: CI workflow files; Semgrep dashboard screenshots.

Assessment Item: Code undergoes peer security review before merge
Comments: Mandatory two-reviewer policy enforced via branch protection.
Description (What this question Actually: Whether a second engineer reviews changes with security in mind before code is merged.

Assessment Item: Snyk (or equivalent) SCA scans run in CI/CD
Comments: Enabled on the two main repos; the long tail of services is not covered yet.
Description (What this question Actually: Whether third-party dependencies are automatically scanned for known vulnerabilities.
Examples / Evidence for teams to referen: Snyk project list; CI logs showing the scan step.

Assessment Item: Dependencies are pinned to fixed versions
Comments: Lockfiles are committed for every service.
Description (What this question Actually: Whether dependency versions are locked to prevent unexpected or malicious updates.

Assessment Item: No known high or critical vulnerabilities in production dependencies
Comments: A handful of high findings remain open, each with a remediation ticket.
Description (What this question Actually: Whether the currently deployed dependency set is free of known high or critical CVEs.
Examples / Evidence for teams to referen: Snyk report filtered to high/critical; remediation tickets.

Comments: No reference architectures exist yet; teams design infrastructure ad-hoc.
Description (What this question Actually: Whether approved, reusable security architecture patterns exist for teams to build against.

Assessment Item: Infrastructure is deployed via IaC (Terraform / Bicep)
Comments: 100% of cloud infrastructure is managed through Terraform with peer-reviewed PRs.
Description (What this question Actually: Whether all cloud infrastructure is provisioned through version-controlled code rather than the console.

Assessment Item: IaC deployment gates block insecure infrastructure changes
Comments: Policy-as-code (OPA / Checkov) blocks non-compliant plans at the deployment gate.
Description (What this question Actually: Whether automated policy gates stop insecure infrastructure from being deployed.
Examples / Evidence for teams to referen: OPA/Checkov policies; pipeline gate failure logs.

Assessment Item: Advanced security metrics are tracked on an executive dashboard
Comments: MTTR, scan coverage, and escaped-defect rate are reported to leadership monthly.
Description (What this question Actually: Whether mature, outcome-based security metrics are tracked and reviewed by leadership.
Examples / Evidence for teams to referen: Executive dashboard screenshots; monthly metrics deck.

Assessment Item: No hardcoded credentials or secrets in source code
Comments: Secret-scanning gate is active and violations break the build.
Description (What this question Actually: Whether secrets are kept out of source control entirely.

Comments: Org-wide push protection plus CI scanning, with alerts triaged within 24 hours.
Description (What this question Actually: Whether automated secret scanning runs on commits and pushes.
Examples / Evidence for teams to referen: GitHub secret-scanning alerts; CI scan logs.

Assessment Item: Secrets are stored in a managed vault (Azure Key Vault / HashiCorp)
Comments: Migration is still in progress; a few legacy jobs read secrets from environment files.
Description (What this question Actually: Whether application secrets are stored in and retrieved from a managed secrets vault.
Examples / Evidence for teams to referen: Key Vault references; vault access policies.

Assessment Item: Credentials are rotated on a regular schedule
Comments: Automated rotation for databases; still manual elsewhere.
Description (What this question Actually: Whether credentials and keys are rotated automatically on a defined cadence.
Examples / Evidence for teams to referen: Rotation runbooks; Key Vault rotation policy.

Assessment Item: Base images are sourced from an approved internal registry
Comments: All Dockerfiles pull from the golden-image registry.
Description (What this question Actually: Whether containers start from hardened, approved base images.

Assessment Item: Container images are scanned for vulnerabilities (Trivy / Grype)
Comments: Scanning runs on the main services; batch jobs are not yet wired in.
Description (What this question Actually: Whether built images are scanned for OS and library CVEs before deploy.

Assessment Item: An SBOM is generated for each image during build
Comments: No SBOM tooling is in place today.
Description (What this question Actually: Whether a software bill of materials is produced for each image.
Examples / Evidence for teams to referen: Syft / CycloneDX output attached to builds.

Assessment Item: Mobile application shielding / RASP is enabled
Comments: This project ships only backend services; there is no mobile client.
Description (What this question Actually: Whether runtime protection is enabled for mobile clients.
Examples / Evidence for teams to referen: N/A - no mobile application in scope.
